phase-scope.phase-31: User-facing backend API and compact mobile dashboard UX for personal MTProto proxy access passed local verification.

commands.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_api.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py -v
commands.command-4: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-31.xml docs/modules/M-045.xml docs/verification/V-M-045.xml docs/verification-index.xml && mygrace lint

verification-levels.module-local: Backend API tests prove owner-only payload delivery, safe failure codes, and no unauthenticated or unverified proxy leakage.
verification-levels.wave-level: M-042 and M-043 tests remain in the gate so dashboard payloads follow assignment persistence, deterministic SNI, and reissue policy truth.
verification-levels.phase-level: Frontend build and mobile smoke must prove the MTProto card fits the compact dashboard without hiding VPN trial/config actions.

module-checks.check-1: Authenticated verified owner can retrieve server, port, secret, tg link, status, and reissue guidance for their own assignment.
module-checks.check-2: Unauthenticated, unverified, or wrong-owner requests cannot retrieve proxy link or secret fields.
module-checks.check-3: Pending, degraded, config-incomplete, and reissue-required states return stable safe codes and user-actionable messages.
module-checks.check-4: Frontend API types preserve the exact backend response shape and do not print secret-bearing payloads to console logs.
module-checks.check-5: Dashboard MTProto card renders open Telegram, copy link, copy server, copy port, and copy secret actions without horizontal scroll.
module-checks.check-6: VPN trial status, subscription CTA, and primary config action remain visible/reachable after adding MTProto dashboard content.

scenarios.scenario-1: Verified user with an active assignment sees activated MTProto status and can open the tg://proxy link.
scenarios.scenario-2: Repeated dashboard loads reuse the canonical assignment and do not create duplicate MTProto rows.
scenarios.scenario-3: Clipboard fallback keeps server, port, and secret visible as selectable manual fields if browser clipboard fails.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unverified user sees locked or pending verification state without trial/VPN/MTProto side effects.
scenarios.scenario-5: Bridge degraded or runtime pending state renders a compact unavailable state and never blocks normal VPN dashboard actions.
scenarios.scenario-6: Reissue-required assignment prompts explicit reissue guidance instead of silently showing stale proxy credentials.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Backend logs may include user id, assignment id, SNI, status, safe failure code, and correlation id.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Backend logs, frontend console logs, smoke traces, and admin summaries must not print full tg://proxy links, derived secrets, base secret, salt, JWT/session tokens, or email verification tokens.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Full link and secret fields may appear only in authenticated owner API responses and owner dashboard UI.

ui-smoke-gates.gate-1: Dashboard at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932 has document scrollWidth <= innerWidth.
ui-smoke-gates.gate-2: MTProto card appears on the main dashboard without pushing VPN subscription/config actions out of practical mobile reach.
ui-smoke-gates.gate-3: All copy/open controls have stable accessible labels and do not resize the dashboard layout after success/error state changes.
ui-smoke-gates.gate-4: No wide table, nested card, hero block, or desktop-only proxy detail layout is introduced.

telemetry-requirements.metric-2: mtproto_owner_payload_failure_count tagged by safe failure code.
telemetry-requirements.metric-3: mtproto_dashboard_card_render_count tagged by UI state without secret-bearing labels.
telemetry-requirements.metric-4: mtproto_copy_action_count tagged by copied field name and browser support status.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-31 can be executed locally with backend API tests, fake/local MTProto provisioning settings, frontend build, and mobile smoke. DNS, TLS, shared 443 edge, nginx, docker-compose, deploy scripts, and install scripts remain out of scope.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: Backend V-M-045/M-042/M-043 pytest gate passed locally with 15 tests.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-14: frontend npm run build passed and generated production assets without tracked dist changes.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-14: scripts/phase31-mtproto-cabinet-smoke.mjs passed, including deploy/install surface guard.
